articles.title: Sensex today | Stock Market Highlights: Markets slip for 4th straight day as crude rises; Sensex drops 363 pts, Nifty ends below 23,870
articles.excerpt:
Sensex Today, Nifty 50 | Stock Market Highlights - Find here all the key highlights related to Sensex, Nifty, BSE, NSE, share prices and Indian stock markets for July 23, 2026.

Market benchmark indices Sensex and Nifty ended lower on Thursday, extending losses for the fourth straight session, as a sharp jump in crude oil prices due to escalating tensions in West Asia weighed on investor sentiment.

The 30-share BSE Sensex declined 363.66 points, or 0.47 per cent, to settle at 76,391.39. During the day, it tanked 603.07 points, or 0.78 per cent, to 76,151.98.

The 50-share NSE Nifty dipped 126.65 points, or 0.53 per cent, to end at 23,869.60.

From the Sensex pack, Adani Ports, Bajaj Finance

articles.title: Rupee ends nearly flat as central bank intervention blunts oil strain
articles.excerpt:
On Thursday, the Indian rupee hovered close to a two-month low, influenced by potential intervention from the central bank to counteract rising oil prices. Brent crude oil approached the $100 per barrel mark following recent Houthi attacks, raising fears about India's economic stability. In light of these developments, investors have increasingly taken short positions against the rupee due to the mounting risks.

articles.title: Indonesia's Danantara plans dollar bond sale worth at least $500 mln, sources say
articles.excerpt:
Danantara, Indonesia's sovereign wealth fund, plans to issue dollar bonds worth $500 million. These long-dated bonds will extend the fund's offshore funding into longer maturities. The issuance aims to match overseas equity investments with long-term USD funding. This move will also reduce pressure on the Indonesian rupiah currency. The fund has hired several banks to manage the potential bond deal.

articles.title: Oil prices scorch Indian bonds; rising Treasury yields add to pressure
articles.excerpt:
Indian government bonds declined early Thursday as Middle East tensions escalated. Crude oil prices neared $100 a barrel, and U.S. Treasury yields also rose. Higher oil prices threaten to revive inflation pressures and weaken the Indian rupee. This situation could significantly impact energy-intensive industries and economies. India imports nearly ninety percent of its crude oil requirements.

articles.title: ICICI Bank sets initial guidance for first dollar bond in nearly 9 years, bankers say
articles.excerpt:
ICICI Bank is returning to dollar debt markets after nine years. The lender seeks to raise at least $500 million through a five-year bond. This issuance leverages the central bank's lower-cost hedging facility. Proceeds will be used for general corporate purposes. The offering will be rated Baa3 by Moody's and BBB by S&P.

articles.title: HDB Financial to reissue 3-year bonds, bankers say
articles.excerpt:
HDB Financial Services is gearing up to secure ten billion rupees via a bond reissue, enticing investors with a yield of seven point seven five percent. Bids for this alluring opportunity will be collected on Wednesday. The offering pertains to a bond issued in July two thousand twenty-nine with a nominal rate of eight point two three zero one percent, and it includes a six billion rupee greenshoe option.

articles.title: HDFC Bank shares slide 10% in four day after Q1 results but brokerages see up to 40% upside. Here’s why
articles.excerpt:
HDFC Bank shares have fallen nearly 10% in four sessions since its Q1 FY27 results despite reporting steady earnings growth. Brokerages remain bullish, citing improving margins, healthy loan growth, lower credit costs and attractive valuations, with target prices implying upside potential of up to 40% from current levels.

articles.title: HDFC Bank shares tumble over 8% in three days on net interest margin concerns
articles.excerpt:
Although HDFC Bank reported a 5% year-on-year increase in June-quarter net profit to ₹19,060 crore and a 7% rise in net interest income, weaker operating profit, lower total income and pressure on margins weighed on investor sentiment. | Photo Credit: ANUSHREE FADNAVIS

Shares of HDFC Bank declined for the third day in a row on Wednesday, falling over 8 per cent and wiping out Rs 1 lakh crore from its market valuation, amid concerns on the margin front.

The stock ended at Rs 753.15, down 1.09 per cent on the BSE. During the day, it lost 1.47 per cent to Rs 750.25.

At the NSE, it declined 1 per cent to end at Rs 753.15.

In three days, the stock tanked 8.11 per cent, wiping out Rs 1 lakh cr
articles.citation: HDFC Bank shares tumble over 8% in three days on net interest margin concerns — BusinessLine Mkts, 2026-07-22. https://www.thehindubusinessline.com/markets/stock-markets/hdfc-bank-shares-tumble-over-8-in-three-days-on-net-interest-margin-concerns/article71253282.ece

articles.title: US-Iran tensions underpin dollar as yen nears 40-year low
articles.excerpt:
The dollar stabilized on Thursday as U.S.-Iran tensions increased investor caution. Rising oil prices fueled inflation fears and supported the safe-haven dollar. The Japanese yen languished near a forty-year low with little recovery in sight. European Central Bank is expected to hold rates steady but may hike later. Australian and New Zealand dollars softened against the strengthening greenback.

articles.title: Yen’s Slump in Trade-Weighted Gauge Shows Its Broad-Based Drop
articles.excerpt:
(Bloomberg) -- While the yen’s plunge to a four-decade low against the dollar has dominated headlines, a broader gauge of the currency’s strength is sending an equally worrying signal for Japan.

The Bank of Japan’s nominal effective exchange rate index — which measures the yen against a trade-weighted basket of currencies — has extended its slide to fresh record lows this year, reflecting the currency’s broad-based weakness against the euro, sterling and several Asian peers.

The decline underscores how the yen’s weakness extends well beyond the dollar, intensifying concerns over imported inflation and Japan’s eroding purchasing power. A weaker trade-weighted yen pushes up the cost of impor
articles.citation: Yen’s Slump in Trade-Weighted Gauge Shows Its Broad-Based Drop — Mint Markets, 2026-07-23. https://www.livemint.com/market/yens-slump-in-trade-weighted-gauge-shows-its-broad-based-drop-11784768496092.html

articles.title: Yen Rebound Ebbs After Brief Rally on Outlook for BOJ Rate Hikes
articles.excerpt:
(Bloomberg) -- The yen slid beyond 163 per dollar for the first time since 1986 before recovering some ground in choppy trading on the prospect of faster interest-rate hikes from the Bank of Japan.

The currency fell to 163.24 per dollar on Tuesday, then briefly recovered to around 162.70 Wednesday after Bloomberg News reported that central bank officials are open to raising rates at a faster pace than the consensus among economists. That bounce faded, with the yen trading around 163.15 as of 4:15 p.m. in New York.

While the BOJ is widely expected to move rates roughly once every six months, it’s open to moving earlier than that timeframe if needed, people familiar with the matter said, poi

articles.title: Broker’s Call: Karur Vysya Bank (Buy)
articles.excerpt:
In Q1 FY27, Karur Vysys Bank’s net interest margin (NIM) was at 4.26 per cent, up 40 bps year on year. The management maintained its prior FY27 NIM guidance in the range of 3.7-3.8 per cent. The management expects NIM to remain above 4 per cent in Q2. The margin (4.26 per cent) excludes the one-off interest income from the income-tax refund, which contributed 8 bps during the quarter. For the quarter, interest component in recovery from written-off accounts was ₹24 crore.

Loan growth, though a tad lower than system, was healthy in the absolute sense: The advances for the bank stood at ₹1,04,090 crore, up 17 per cent y-o-y. The bank had earlier guided that they would grow 100-200 bps above b
articles.citation: Broker’s Call: Karur Vysya Bank (Buy) — BusinessLine Mkts, 2026-07-21. https://www.thehindubusinessline.com/markets/brokers-call-karur-vysya-bank-buy/article71248725.ece

articles.title: Sotefin Bharat IPO listing: Shares hit upper circuit after listing at a 10% premium; details here
articles.excerpt:
Sotefin Bharat IPO listing: Sotefin Bharat share price made a positive debut on the BSE SME on Thursday, 23 July, and extended gains to hit its upper circuit despite weak market sentiment.

The SME stock opened at ₹205, up 9.6% from the issue price of ₹187, and jumped further to hit the 5% upper price band of ₹215.25. Around 10:30 AM, the stock was locked at its upper circuit, which is 15% up from the issue price, while equity benchmark Sensex was 109 points, or 0.14%, down at 76,646.

The listing of Sotefin Bharat shares exceeded grey-market expectations, with the last grey-market premium (GMP) at ₹2.50, suggesting the stock could list at ₹189.50, a premium of 1.34% over the issue price.

articles.title: Sotefin Bharat shares set for BSE SME debut today. GMP signals muted listing
articles.excerpt:
Sotefin Bharat is set to debut on the BSE SME platform on July 23, with grey market premium (GMP) trends indicating a largely flat listing. Despite subdued GMP, the Rs 89.76-crore IPO was subscribed 3.78 times overall, backed by healthy participation across investor categories. The company plans to use the IPO proceeds to expand manufacturing capacity, strengthen operations, and meet working capital needs.

articles.title: Bharat Coking Coal shares fall 7% after Q1FY27 net loss
articles.excerpt:
Coal India arm Bharat Coking Coal shares fell 7 per cent after the company reported a net loss of ₹68.09 crore in Q1FY27, compared with a profit of ₹176.87 crore in the same quarter last year.

The stock closed at ₹34.92 on the NSE (down 6.90 per cent), hitting a low of ₹34.40 from the previous close of ₹37.51.

Revenue from operations declined 3.5 per cent to ₹3,587.27 crore in Q1FY27 from ₹3,719.59 crore in the corresponding quarter last year.
